_notes: CAPABILITY MATRIX — what each model CAN'T do natively (pdf/image/web/youtube/file_read/email_send/structured_output). The brain only DECLARES capability; it never fulfills (Rule #9). A connected app reads this, and when the active model LACKS a needed capability it routes to its own integration (e.g. PDF → headless Chrome) — or alerts if that fulfillment is unconfigured. Matching mirrors models.json: by substring against the lowercased model id; first match wins; unknown → `default` (lacks everything risky, safe). `has`/`lacks` reference capability keys defined in `capabilities`.

capabilities.pdf: Generate a PDF artifact (a brief, a deck) directly.
capabilities.web: Fetch live web pages / browse during the turn (vs. training-only).
capabilities.file_read: Read local files and follow [[links]] itself (agentic) vs. needing a pre-assembled pack.
capabilities.email_send: Send email directly (no model does this natively — always an app integration).

models.label: Claude
models.notes: Agentic + JSON-strong with live web (tool use), but cannot natively render a PDF or generate images — the connected app fulfills those.

models.label: Gemini
models.notes: Native image + YouTube + web. Cannot render a PDF — the connected app fulfills.

models.label: ChatGPT / Codex
models.notes: Agentic + image-capable, but web is OFF by default in the CLI — treat as lacking web unless the app enables a fetch tool.

models.label: Local (open-weight)
models.notes: Raw local model over an API: JSON via constrained decoding only. Cannot read files, browse, or make media — the connected app must pre-assemble and fulfill every capability.

models.label: Gamma
models.notes: Example: builds decks/pages but cannot generate a PDF artifact — the connected app fulfills PDF (headless Chrome).

default.label: Unknown model
default.notes: Unrecognized — assume it can do nothing risky natively. The connected app must fulfill or refuse every capability. Safe for any new model.
